LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LUXIM CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST JONES DAY CONTINUED FROM SEP-17-2007 TO LAW AND MOTION AT OCT-10-2007, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MATTER IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 10, 2007 AT 9:30 A.M, ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. THE PARTIES ARE TO PREPARE A SINGLE DOCUMENT THAT REFLECTS THE MOVING PARTY'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE AND THE OPPOSITION OF THE PROPONENT OF THE EVIDENCE. EACH OBJECTION AND OPPOSITION SHALL BE ON A SINGLE PAGE. THE TEXT IS TO BE DOUBLE SPACED, AND DIVIDED EQUALLY BETWEEN THE PARTIES USING LINES 1 THROUGH 24. LINES 25 TO 28 SHALL INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING: ON LINE 25, SET FORTH A LINE FOR THE COURT TO CHECK "GRANTED" OR "DENIED" OR "GRANTED IN PART/DENIED IN PART". LINES 27 AND 28 ARE TO CONTAIN A SINGLE LINE ON EACH FOR THE COURT TO EXPLAIN, IF NECESSARY, THE RULING. THIS DOCUMENT SHALL BE FILED WITH THE COURT ON OCTOBER 2. 2007. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LUXIM CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST JONES DAY. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIES THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 17, 2007. COURT ORDERS THAT BOTH SIDES ARE TO FILE SEPARATE PLEADINGS THAT POINTS TO WHATEVER EVIDENCE THEY BELIEVE EXISTS AS ACTION TAKEN BY JONES DAY OUTSIDE OF THE PTO CASE. PROVIDE FURTHER DISTINCTIONS BETWEEN THE U. K. PROCEEDINGS AND "OTHER ASSISTANTS" TO JONES DAY ATTORNEY. BASED ON STIPULATION OF COUNSEL, THEY ARE TO MEET AND CONFER RE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS AND THEN SUBMIT AN ABBREVIATED SET OF EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO THE COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF LUXIM CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST JONES DAY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS OF COURT'S RULING) LUXIM CORPORATION HAS FILED CERTAIN DOCUMENTS PROVISIONALLY UNDER SEAL. IT APPEARS THAT THOSE DOCUMENTS ARE CONFIDENTIAL ATTORNEY-CLIENT COMMUNICATIONS AND/OR ARE PROTECTED BY THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THEY ARE PROTECTED UNDER CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 2.550 AND 2.551 AND MAY BE FILED UNDER SEAL. NO OTHER PORTION OF THE RECORD NEED BE SEALED. LUXIM IS DIRECTED TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT CONFORMS TO RULE 2.551(E). ORDER ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS SIGNED AND FILED THIS DATE; COPIES OF COURT'S ORDER GIVEN TO COUNSEL IN OPEN COURT. ORDER SIGNED AND FILED DENYING THE PETITION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT AN ORDER REGARDING THE SEALING OF DOCUMENTS. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

Ntc And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against Jones Day
Off Calendar Dec-19-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF LUXIM CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST JONES DAY.
APPEARANCES: PAMELA A. PHILLIPS, ESQUIRE (HOWARD RICE, 415.217.5910) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ROBERT A. MITTELSTAEDT, ESQUIRE (JONES DAY, 415.626.3939) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: The motion for a preliminary injunction is denied. The focus of the alleged misuse of confidential information, based on a presumption as contrasted with fact, is centered in proceedings before the federal Patent and Trademark Office ("PTO") and in litigation in England. The court does not have the authority to determine who may practice before the PTO or the application of the rules of practice before the PTO. The same would appear to be true as to the litigation in England although the parties have not addressed that question. Given the facts presented, it is the Court's conclusion that as a matter of comity it is not appropriate for the Court to grant the relief requested. In so ruling, the Court relies on the decision in In Re Complex Litigation (1991) 232 Cal.App.3d 572, 600-601 and CCP 1061.
Luxim Corporation has filed certain documents provisionally under seal. It appears that those documents are confidential attorney-client communications and/or are protected by the work product doctrine. As a consequence, they are protected under California Rules of Court 2.550 and 2.551 and may be filed under seal. No other portion of the record need be sealed. Luxim is directed to prepare a form of order that conforms to Rule 2.551(e). ORDER ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS SIGNED AND FILED THIS DATE; COPIES OF COURT'S ORDER GIVEN TO COUNSEL IN OPEN COURT. ORDER SIGNED AND FILED DENYING THE PETITION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT AN ORDER REGARDING THE SEALING OF DOCUMENTS. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF LUXIM CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST JONES DAY.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 10, 2007 AT 9:30 A.M, ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. THE PARTIES ARE TO PREPARE A SINGLE DOCUMENT THAT REFLECTS THE MOVING PARTY'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE AND THE OPPOSITION OF THE PROPONENT OF THE EVIDENCE. EACH OBJECTION AND OPPOSITION SHALL BE ON A SINGLE PAGE. THE TEXT IS TO BE DOUBLE SPACED, AND DIVIDED EQUALLY BETWEEN THE PARTIES USING LINES 1 THROUGH 24. LINES 25 TO 28 SHALL INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING: ON LINE 25, SET FORTH A LINE FOR THE COURT TO CHECK "GRANTED" OR "DENIED" OR "GRANTED IN PART/DENIED IN PART". LINES 27 AND 28 ARE TO CONTAIN A SINGLE LINE ON EACH FOR THE COURT TO EXPLAIN, IF NECESSARY, THE RULING. THIS DOCUMENT SHALL BE FILED WITH THE COURT ON OCTOBER 2. 2007. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
